expectations.text: Identifies the issue as ambiguous spacing or unclear proximity between related elements
expectations.evidence: 'The Core Problem: Proximity. The visual distance between a label and its input should be smaller than the distance between consecutive form groups. If all gaps are equal, the eye can't group them.'

expectations.text: Explains that related elements (label + input) should be closer to each other than to unrelated elements
expectations.evidence: 'Use the law of proximity deliberately...Label→Input: close (4–6px gap); larger gap between groups (24–32px)'

expectations.text: Gives specific spacing values for label-to-input (small) vs group-to-group (larger)
expectations.evidence: 'Label → Input gap: 4px–8px; Input → Next label gap: 20px–32px' with CSS: `label { margin-bottom: 4px }`, `.form-group { margin-bottom: 24px }`

suggestions.reason: Spacing proximity is well-covered by general LLM knowledge. Consider tightening assertions to require Refactoring UI-specific framing (e.g., 'spacing scale' recommendation) to better differentiate skill vs no-skill responses

eval_feedback.overall: Both with_skill and without_skill pass all assertions — the spacing proximity principle is well-covered by general design knowledge and may not be a discriminating eval.
